# Security Guardrails
<!-- Generated by handover v1.2.1 -->

## Data residency

- No source code, symbols, logs, or observations may be sent to any external service unless the user has explicitly configured an external provider and the command includes a visible confirmation
- All SQLite databases are stored under `.context-router/` in the repo root or workspace root; never in system-wide locations without user consent

## Secret masking

- Before writing any observation, log line, or stack trace to storage, run it through a secret masker that redacts: API keys (common patterns), JWT tokens, connection strings, passwords in URLs, `.env` file content
- Secret masker must be applied in the runtime ingestion layer before RuntimeSignal is persisted
- Provide a `context-router secrets test --input <file>` command for users to verify masking

## Ignore patterns

- Respect `.context-routerignore` at repo level and workspace level
- Default ignore list must include: `.env`, `*.pem`, `*.key`, `*.p12`, `*secret*`, `*credential*`, `node_modules/`, `__pycache__/`, `.git/`
- Workspace-level ignore patterns override repo-level patterns

## Optional external providers

- API keys for optional features (LLM summarization, embeddings) must be stored in environment variables, never in config files committed to the repo
- Log a visible warning when an external provider is active: `[context-router] external provider active: <name>`

## Audit log

- All writes to the memory/observation/decision store must be logged with timestamp, command, and file scope to `.context-router/audit.log`
- Audit log is append-only and must never be automatically deleted by any command

## Dependency hygiene

- Pin all production dependencies with hashes in `requirements.lock`
- Run `pip-audit` or equivalent in CI to detect known vulnerabilities in dependencies
